coin_profile.name: Internet Computer
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.
coin_profile.description: Internet Computer (ICP) — a Cash-Flow Protocol ranked #58 by market cap.

dilution_control.label: Dilution Control
dilution_control.chart_ref: Network Economics → Token Supply: Issuance vs Burns

moat.rationale: ICP has a novel technical moat through its smart contract capabilities and chain fusion architecture, but faces significant headwinds: weak network effects (low developer adoption relative to L1s), poor token value capture (holders receive minimal economic benefits despite fee generation), and limited ecosystem lock-in compared to established layers. The protocol's 4/10 maturity score and modest network demand (4/10) reflect limited proven product-market fit, placing it between governance tokens (ARB/OP at 4) and stronger L1s, with regulatory uncertainty around its governance model adding additional risk to long-term defensibility.

chart_zones.mos_note: Margin of safety present — price near or below speculative fair value during accumulation (deep bear) phase.

2026.net_issuance_note: Current snapshot only. Historical supply data requires on-chain indexing not available via free APIs.

meta.data_note: No protocol fee or TVL data found for this token on DeFiLlama. Charts 1 and 2 are unavailable. Charts 3 and 4 show current-year supply and dilution data from CoinGecko.

forecast_meta.growth_basis_note: Annual growth rates derived from quality score 5.3/10 and survivability 63.3%. Base: 7.0% p.a., Bull: 13.0% p.a., Bear: -21.1% p.a.
forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? More activity usually means more value. (2) Is the market cap cheap or expensive compared to the fees it earns? (3) What price would make sense given its size rank? The Speculative Fair Value is the average of whichever checks produced a sensible result.
forecast_meta.growth_explanation: Base (7.0%/yr): projected growth based on this token's quality score (5.3/10). Bull (13.0%/yr): adoption accelerates and network usage grows strongly. Bear (-21.1%/yr): adoption slows or competition erodes the token's position. The bear scenario is steeper than usual because survivability is only 63.3% — this token has weaker dilution control or holder value accrual, making it more vulnerable in a downturn.
forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, and Holder Revenue are unavailable: this token has no DeFiLlama fee tracking. Valuation relies entirely on network activity and market positioning signals. Network Activity (NVT) treats 24h trading volume as a proxy for on-chain utility — it fires when volume is available and the resulting IV falls within 0.1x–10x of current price. Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment did not fire: FDV/MC is within the acceptable range for this quality tier, so no dilution penalty was applied. Only 2 methods contributed to the median. More data sources (DeFiLlama fee history) would improve confidence in this estimate. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.

watchlist_signal.reason: Quality Cash-Flow Protocol with a margin of safety present — price near speculative fair value during a constructive cycle phase.

Continued development activity and ecosystem growth support a constructive long-term view

Long-term execution risk remains a key variable for any crypto investment

metric_assessments.fee_growth: ICP scores 4/10 because fee generation data is not yet publicly available, leaving no measurable track record of how effectively the protocol monetizes network activity. The protocol's infrastructure-as-a-service model is theoretically positioned to accumulate fees as adoption grows, but without transparent, auditable fee metrics, investors cannot verify whether the economic model is actually functioning. Without clarity on fee sustainability or growth trajectory, confidence in this component remains limited.
metric_assessments.capital_efficiency: ICP scores 5/10 because it ranks #58 by market cap but has not yet demonstrated outsized returns on deployed capital relative to competing infrastructure platforms. The protocol operates a meaningful computational network, suggesting capital is being put to productive use, but the modest market position and relatively flat 30-day price action indicate the market hasn't yet recognized exceptional efficiency gains. Until transaction volume or network utility scales materially relative to the capital base, this score reflects the 'show me' stage of deployment.
metric_assessments.holder_value_accrual: ICP scores 5/10 because the protocol lacks transparent fee-sharing or token burn mechanisms that would directly distribute protocol earnings to holders. The architectural design includes staking incentives and governance participation, creating some alignment between holders and protocol health, but these indirect benefits pale compared to protocols with explicit profit-sharing or deflationary tokenomics. Without a clear path for holders to capture compounding value from network growth, value accrual remains speculative rather than structural.
metric_assessments.network_demand: ICP scores 4/10 with a 4.21% volume-to-market-cap ratio, which indicates modest on-chain activity relative to its $1.4B valuation. The protocol supports meaningful computation and hosting use cases, showing real utility, but the ratio suggests the network has not yet attracted the sustained developer or user demand needed to generate organic fee pressure. The gap between theoretical infrastructure potential and demonstrated actual usage is the primary constraint on this score.
metric_assessments.dilution_control: ICP scores 10/10 because 100% of tokens are already in circulation with no further dilution ahead—the FDV-to-MC ratio is 1.0, meaning there is no hidden overhang of future supply. This is a structural strength that protects existing holders from token issuance risk and ensures scarcity is locked in. For a speculative-stage protocol, this clarity on supply eliminates one major source of downside volatility.
metric_assessments.protocol_maturity: ICP scores 4/10 because its rank of #58 reflects an infrastructure protocol that has built a functioning network but remains well outside the top tier of ecosystem adoption or network effects. The protocol has weathered several years of development and real-world deployment, demonstrating technical competence, but it has not yet reached the scale or cultural entrenchment of leading Layer 1 platforms. Maturity in this context means the protocol works, but the market is still deciding whether it will become essential infrastructure.

quality_narrative.classification_reason: Internet Computer is rated Speculative because its quality score of 5.3/10 reflects a protocol with functional infrastructure but unproven economic moats and fee generation mechanisms. While dilution control is excellent and the network is technically operational, gaps in fee monetization visibility, modest network demand relative to valuation, and mid-tier market ranking create meaningful uncertainty about whether holders will see proportional returns as the network scales. The absence of transparent fee metrics and value accrual pathways leaves this investment dependent on future adoption execution rather than current demonstrated fundamentals.
quality_narrative.investment_narrative: Internet Computer is a decentralized computing infrastructure protocol attempting to host websites, applications, and services directly on blockchain rather than traditional cloud providers—a bold but unproven thesis at scale. The protocol's greatest strength is its completed token supply (100% circulating) which eliminates dilution risk, and it has built a functioning network capable of real computational work, but the critical weakness is the lack of visible, compounding fee generation or mechanisms that reward token holders as the network grows. Network activity (4.21% volume-to-MC) is modest for a $1.4B valuation, suggesting the market remains unconvinced about near-term utility expansion or developer adoption. At this stage, ICP is a high-risk bet on whether decentralized computing becomes genuinely necessary infrastructure or remains a niche use case; it is most suitable for investors comfortable with multi-year uncertainty and the possibility that the protocol's architectural bet does not gain mainstream traction.
quality_narrative.quality_conclusion: Internet Computer scores 5.3/10 (Speculative) as a Cash-Flow Protocol. The overall quality profile reflects the protocol's maturity, fee economics, and token supply dynamics relative to peers.

moat.rationale: IOTA lacks a defensible moat despite its DAG-based technical differentiation; the protocol has struggled with adoption (rank 170, $0.2B market cap), shows weak fundamental quality signals across fee growth, network demand, and protocol maturity (all scoring 2-4), and offers minimal token utility with no established value capture for holders. The low holder value accrual (5/10) and capital efficiency (5/10) indicate the network hasn't achieved meaningful economic traction, while its young maturity (2/10) and lack of ecosystem lock-in make it vulnerable to forking and competition from more established L1s and DLT protocols.

metric_assessments.fee_growth: IOTA scores 4/10 because the protocol shows moderate fee generation relative to its market cap, with a volume-to-market-cap proxy of 3.11%, indicating transaction activity exists but isn't exceptional. The protocol is capturing some economic value through its network operations, which is a positive foundation. However, the score is capped by the absence of clear fee acceleration or a transparent fee mechanism that would justify a higher rating—there's activity, but no signal of growing monetization.
metric_assessments.capital_efficiency: IOTA scores 5/10 based on its rank proxy positioning, suggesting it's deploying capital at an average efficiency level within its peer group. The protocol maintains a functional network that processes transactions, which demonstrates baseline capital productivity. The limiting factor is that at rank 170, IOTA hasn't achieved the capital deployment efficiency of higher-ranked peers, meaning its market position hasn't yet justified its valuation through outsized returns on deployed resources.
metric_assessments.holder_value_accrual: IOTA scores 5/10 on estimated holder value accrual, indicating the protocol generates some return pathways for token holders but lacks a strong or transparent value capture mechanism. The protocol likely distributes some benefits through staking or network participation, but these mechanisms appear modest relative to the market cap. Without a clear, compelling fee-sharing or buyback structure, holders aren't seeing concentrated value accrual that would elevate this score.
metric_assessments.network_demand: IOTA scores 4/10 with a volume-to-market-cap ratio of 3.11%, which reflects moderate but not compelling network utilization relative to its $249M valuation. The protocol is seeing real usage, which validates its practical purpose, but the ratio suggests transaction demand hasn't scaled to create outsized network effects. This indicates IOTA remains in an early or niche adoption phase rather than a breakout demand phase.
metric_assessments.dilution_control: IOTA scores 8/10 with 90.9% of its token supply already in circulation, meaning the protocol has minimal dilution risk ahead and token holders face limited future supply pressure. This is a genuine strength—the supply is largely fixed, which protects existing holders from dilution eroding their stake. The score isn't perfect because the high circulating percentage also means the protocol has already minted most of its tokens, leaving little flexibility for future incentive mechanisms or ecosystem development.
metric_assessments.protocol_maturity: IOTA scores 2/10 at rank 170, indicating it lags significantly behind established, higher-ranked protocols in market adoption and proven utility. While IOTA has been operational and has a technical foundation, its market position hasn't advanced to the tier of proven, widely-adopted infrastructure. The low score reflects that maturity isn't just about age—it's about proving consistent market relevance, and at rank 170, IOTA hasn't yet demonstrated that at scale.

quality_narrative.classification_reason: IOTA receives a Dangerous rating because its overall quality score of 4.4/10 (44.2th percentile) reveals critical weaknesses that outweigh its single strength in dilution control. Protocol maturity scores only 2/10—a severe red flag—indicating the project lacks the market traction and proven track record of tier-1 protocols, while fee growth, capital efficiency, and holder value accrual all cluster at 4-5/10, showing no exceptional moat or compelling tokenomics. The combination of low adoption signals (3.11% volume-to-MC) and unproven value accrual mechanisms means investors bear significant execution risk with limited downside protection.
quality_narrative.investment_narrative: IOTA is a utility-focused protocol attempting to build a decentralized network for real-world applications, but it remains immature and unproven at rank 170 with only 3.11% network volume-to-market-cap ratio. The protocol's main strength is its nearly complete token supply (90.9% circulating), which eliminates dilution risk going forward, but this benefit is overshadowed by weak fee generation, unclear holder value mechanisms, and—most critically—a lack of market validation that its technology or adoption thesis is working at scale. The core risk is that despite years of development, IOTA hasn't built sufficient network demand or demonstrated a compelling economic moat to justify its $249M valuation against more established competitors. Token supply dynamics are neutral rather than helpful—while dilution is controlled, there's no active incentive structure driving demand. This is a speculative bet on a team and vision rather than a protocol with proven cash flow or user demand, making it suitable only for investors with high risk tolerance and a multi-year conviction thesis.
quality_narrative.quality_conclusion: IOTA scores 4.4/10 (Dangerous) as a Cash-Flow Protocol. The overall quality profile reflects the protocol's maturity, fee economics, and token supply dynamics relative to peers.

moat.rationale: IP shows weak protocol moat with critical vulnerabilities across most dimensions. The 65.5% uncirculated supply poses extreme dilution risk, token utility is underdeveloped, and without demonstrated network effects or switching costs, the protocol is highly forked/replaceable and lacks meaningful ecosystem lock-in typical of defensible protocols.

metric_assessments.fee_growth: Story scores 6/10 on fee growth, but this is measured as a proxy rather than actual fee data—the protocol doesn't publish clear annual fee metrics, making this assessment educated guesswork. The score suggests the protocol is generating some economic activity relative to its market cap, but without transparent fee reporting, there's no way to verify whether this revenue is growing, stable, or even sustainable. This opacity prevents a higher score and raises questions about whether the protocol is genuinely capturing value or simply showing trading volume churn.
metric_assessments.capital_efficiency: A score of 5/10 reflects that Story ranks #204 by market cap—solid enough to be in the top 200, but not exceptional for a cash-flow protocol expected to generate shareholder returns. The protocol hasn't demonstrated capital deployment that visibly outperforms peers at similar market valuations, suggesting its investors haven't yet achieved breakthrough returns from the capital deployed. Without stronger efficiency metrics or clear revenue-per-dollar-of-capital, Story sits in the middle of the pack with room to prove its economic model works.
metric_assessments.holder_value_accrual: Story scores 5/10 on holder value accrual, estimated rather than measured by concrete buybacks, dividends, or token burns—meaning there's limited evidence the protocol is systematically returning value to token holders. The protocol may have mechanisms in place, but they appear modest relative to its market cap, and the lack of transparent reporting suggests value accrual isn't a core focus yet. Without demonstrated, measurable ways that holding IP creates ongoing wealth, investors are essentially betting on price appreciation rather than protocol-generated returns.
metric_assessments.network_demand: A strong 8/10 score reflects that Story commands 17.11% volume-to-market-cap ratio, indicating the network is actively being used relative to its size—significantly higher than most protocols at this market cap. This suggests real demand for the protocol's services and user engagement that translates to transaction activity, a genuine positive signal. However, even robust volume-to-cap ratios don't guarantee the protocol captures margin on that activity or converts it to sustainable fees, so demand alone doesn't solve Story's transparency and profitability questions.
metric_assessments.dilution_control: Story scores only 2/10 because 65.5% of tokens remain unlocked (measured by 34.5% circulating), with an FDV-to-MC ratio of 2.89 indicating massive future supply pressure. This means existing holders face substantial dilution as vesting schedules release tokens, which will likely put downward pressure on price regardless of network demand. Without aggressive buyback programs or burning mechanisms, early investors face a heavy headwind against long-term price appreciation simply from token economics alone.
metric_assessments.protocol_maturity: Story's rank #204 earns only 1/10 for protocol maturity, confirming this is an early-stage project competing in a crowded ecosystem without yet achieving proven longevity or dominance. Early rank doesn't inherently mean the protocol is doomed, but it does mean Story lacks the track record, network effects, or market position of proven cash-flow protocols like Aave or Curve. At this stage, Story remains unproven relative to competitors, and many projects at this rank eventually fade rather than scale.

quality_narrative.classification_reason: Story receives a Dangerous rating because it combines extreme token dilution (2.89 FDV-to-MC ratio with only 34.5% circulating) with early-stage maturity (#204 rank), unverified fee economics, and modest capital efficiency—creating a high-risk profile for retail investors. While network demand (8/10) shows the protocol has real usage, this strength is overwhelmed by the structural headwinds: massive future dilution will suppress price, there's no proof the protocol is profitable or returning value to holders, and it lacks the maturity or scale to justify its current valuation. The combination of unfavorable token mechanics and unproven business model puts downside risk significantly ahead of upside opportunity.
quality_narrative.investment_narrative: Story is an early-stage cash-flow protocol (#204 rank) with genuine network demand—its 17.11% volume-to-cap ratio shows real user engagement—but the economics don't yet support holding the token. The protocol lacks transparent fee reporting, doesn't visibly return value to shareholders, and faces crushing dilution ahead as 65.5% of tokens remain locked (2.89 FDV-to-MC). While the underlying protocol may be useful and see increased adoption, the token structure is designed to penalize current holders: dilution will likely suppress price for years regardless of business success, and without proven profitability or value accrual mechanisms, you're betting purely on price appreciation in a crowded field. For beginners, Story is a speculative bet on execution in a space full of better-capitalized and more mature alternatives—avoid unless you have high risk tolerance and conviction the team can achieve exceptional growth.
quality_narrative.quality_conclusion: Story scores 4.3/10 (Dangerous) as a Cash-Flow Protocol. The overall quality profile reflects the protocol's maturity, fee economics, and token supply dynamics relative to peers.

coin_profile.name: Immutable
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.
coin_profile.description: Immutable (IMX) — a Cash-Flow Protocol ranked #239 by market cap.

dilution_control.label: Dilution Control
dilution_control.chart_ref: Network Economics → Token Supply: Issuance vs Burns

moat.rationale: IMX is a gaming-focused L2 with weak moat fundamentals: low protocol maturity (1/10), poor dilution control (2/10), and limited holder value accrual despite being a cash-flow protocol. While it has differentiated gaming positioning, it faces the same L2 commoditization risk as ARB/OP (users follow cheapest fees), lacks meaningful ecosystem lock-in, and can be forked; the 57.88% unvested supply poses significant dilution headwinds that undermine any competitive advantages.

forecast_meta.growth_basis_note: Annual growth rates derived from quality score 4.0/10 and survivability 26.7%. Base: 3.0% p.a., Bull: 9.0% p.a., Bear: -37.0% p.a.
forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? More activity usually means more value. (2) Is the market cap cheap or expensive compared to the fees it earns? (3) What price would make sense given its size rank? The Speculative Fair Value is the average of whichever checks produced a sensible result.
forecast_meta.growth_explanation: Base (3.0%/yr): projected growth based on this token's quality score (4.0/10). Bull (9.0%/yr): adoption accelerates and network usage grows strongly. Bear (-37.0%/yr): adoption slows or competition erodes the token's position. The bear scenario is steeper than usual because survivability is only 26.7% — this token has weaker dilution control or holder value accrual, making it more vulnerable in a downturn.
forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, and Holder Revenue are unavailable: DeFiLlama has no fee data for this token. This typically means the protocol does not generate trackable on-chain fees, or its fee reporting is not yet indexed. Network Activity (NVT) treats 24h trading volume as a proxy for on-chain utility — it fires when volume is available and the resulting IV falls within 0.1x–10x of current price. Metcalfe Network Value is shown in the breakdown chart without contributing to the median: the implied IV is below 10% of current price, indicating the token is trading at a significant premium to its network activity level. Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment did not fire: FDV/MC is within the acceptable range for this quality tier, so no dilution penalty was applied. Only 2 methods contributed to the median. More data sources (DeFiLlama fee history) would improve confidence in this estimate. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.

metric_assessments.fee_growth: Immutable scores 6/10 on fee growth, benefiting from measurable volume-to-market-cap activity that suggests users are actually transacting on the protocol rather than holding idle tokens. The score reflects that trading velocity exists and fees are being generated, which is a prerequisite for any cash-flow protocol to matter. However, the lack of disclosed annual fee data prevents confirmation of whether this activity is translating into meaningful, sustainable revenue capture—making this a ceiling-limited score despite positive signals.
metric_assessments.capital_efficiency: The score of 5/10 places Immutable in the middle tier by rank-proxy standards, meaning it's deploying capital reasonably well relative to peers at similar market cap levels. What's working is that the protocol hasn't accumulated massive TVL or infrastructure overhead relative to its ranking, avoiding the trap of bloated systems. The constraint is that without stronger fee or staking mechanisms, capital deployment remains passive—there's no clear evidence the protocol is compressing its operational footprint or improving returns on deployed assets.
metric_assessments.holder_value_accrual: A score of 5/10 indicates minimal direct token utility for holders through staking or validator rewards, which is typical for gaming and NFT infrastructure plays like Immutable. The protocol likely distributes value indirectly—through ecosystem growth or token burn mechanisms—rather than through clear, quantifiable holder returns. This middling score reflects that while the token exists within a functioning network, there's no compelling financial mechanism pulling value back to token holders consistently.
metric_assessments.network_demand: At 9.41% volume-to-market-cap, Immutable shows legitimate daily trading activity relative to its $136M market cap, earning a 6/10 score that signals real user engagement on the platform. This is the protocol's strongest signal: actual transactions and demand are flowing through the network, which validates that the underlying product (likely gaming or NFT infrastructure) has utility beyond speculation. The limitation is that 9.41% volume, while respectable, isn't exceptional—it suggests a modest, not explosive, user base or transaction intensity.
metric_assessments.dilution_control: The 42.12% circulating supply against a 2.37x fully diluted valuation gap earns only a 2/10 score, reflecting severe token overhang risk for current holders. Nearly 58% of total supply remains locked or unvested, meaning future dilution is a near-certainty as these tokens enter circulation, creating a structural headwind for price appreciation. This is a critical weakness: even if the protocol executes well, token supply expansion will mechanically suppress holder returns unless demand grows faster than supply inflation.
metric_assessments.protocol_maturity: Ranked #239 by market cap, Immutable receives a 1/10 maturity score—it's simply too small and too new relative to established protocols to claim institutional-grade maturity or battle-tested stability. Most protocols reaching top 100 have years of proven uptime, deeper liquidity, and ecosystem moats; Immutable hasn't yet demonstrated it can sustain relevance through market cycles. This rank reflects where the market currently values the protocol's stage, not a death sentence, but it signals investors are still in early-stage conviction territory.

quality_narrative.classification_reason: Immutable is classified as Dangerous because it combines a mediocre overall quality score (4.0/10, only 39.9th percentile) with severe structural weaknesses that outweigh its positive signals. The catastrophic 2/10 dilution control score—driven by 2.37x fully diluted value and only 42% circulating supply—creates a mathematical headwind that no amount of protocol utility can overcome in the near term. Add a 1/10 maturity rank (position #239) indicating the protocol is still unproven at scale, and the risk-reward skews heavily toward downside, especially if market conditions tighten or investor appetite for speculative gaming tokens fades.
quality_narrative.investment_narrative: Immutable is a gaming and NFT infrastructure protocol with legitimate on-chain demand (9.41% volume-to-MC ratio), suggesting its underlying product serves real users rather than existing purely as a token speculation vehicle. The fundamental strength lies in network activity and a use case (gaming/NFTs) that has survived multiple market cycles, indicating some durable product-market fit. However, the protocol is undermined by two fatal structural problems: massive future dilution (only 42% of tokens in circulation, creating a 2.37x overhang) and early-stage maturity (#239 rank), meaning token holders face near-certain supply inflation while the protocol's long-term viability remains unproven. At $0.162, the price reflects this risk reality—down 7% over 30 days—and unless Immutable can accelerate user growth and fee capture dramatically faster than token dilution, current holders are likely subsidizing future users. Investors seeking exposure to gaming infrastructure should wait for either a smaller FDV multiple, higher quality scores, or clear evidence of fee-based value accrual before accumulating.
quality_narrative.quality_conclusion: Immutable scores 4.0/10 (Dangerous) as a Cash-Flow Protocol. The overall quality profile reflects the protocol's maturity, fee economics, and token supply dynamics relative to peers.

coin_profile.name: Irys
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.
coin_profile.description: Irys (IRYS) — a Cash-Flow Protocol ranked #411 by market cap.

IRYS.name: Irys

dilution_control.label: Dilution Control
dilution_control.chart_ref: Network Economics → Token Supply: Issuance vs Burns

chart_zones.mos_note: Price above speculative fair value — no margin of safety.

2026.net_issuance_note: Current snapshot only. Historical supply data requires on-chain indexing not available via free APIs.

meta.data_note: No protocol fee or TVL data found for this token on DeFiLlama. Charts 1 and 2 are unavailable. Charts 3 and 4 show current-year supply and dilution data from CoinGecko.

forecast_meta.growth_basis_note: Annual growth rates derived from quality score 3.8/10 and survivability 23.3%. Base: 3.0% p.a., Bull: 9.0% p.a., Bear: -48.1% p.a.
forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? More activity usually means more value. (2) Is the market cap cheap or expensive compared to the fees it earns? (3) What price would make sense given its size rank? The Speculative Fair Value is the average of whichever checks produced a sensible result.
forecast_meta.growth_explanation: Base (3.0%/yr): projected growth based on this token's quality score (3.8/10). Bull (9.0%/yr): adoption accelerates and network usage grows strongly. Bear (-48.1%/yr): adoption slows or competition erodes the token's position. The bear scenario is steeper than usual because survivability is only 23.3% — this token has weaker dilution control or holder value accrual, making it more vulnerable in a downturn.
forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, and Holder Revenue are unavailable: this token has no DeFiLlama fee tracking. Valuation relies entirely on network activity and market positioning signals. Network Activity (NVT) treats 24h trading volume as a proxy for on-chain utility — it fires when volume is available and the resulting IV falls within 0.1x–10x of current price. Metcalfe Network Value is shown in the breakdown chart without contributing to the median: the implied IV is below 10% of current price, indicating the token is trading at a significant premium to its network activity level. Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment penalises the large gap between circulating and fully-diluted supply — future token unlocks are expected to create selling pressure. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.
forecast_meta.price_history_note: Limited price history available (1 year(s)). This token may be recently listed or have insufficient on-chain data.

IRYS.iv_method: Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, Dilution Adjustment
